name: ds-form-field
description: Wraps a label and a form control, owns error display and validation timing. The P4c behavioral-token showcase: reads --ctx-validation-mode live to decide whether blur or submit triggers validation, with zero per-context branching in its own code.

properties.name: label
properties.description: Field label text, mirrored to the slotted ds-label's accessible name.

properties.name: error
properties.description: Message shown in the error part when validation fails.

events.name: ds-validate
events.description: Fired whenever validation runs (blur in eager mode, or an explicit validate() call), naming the mode it ran under.

constraints.rationale: No primitive references, no raw values.
The error message's color must clear AA against the surface in both contexts; the field wrapper itself is never a positive-tabindex focus stop.
Adaptive class: API drift is structural drift.
allowedChildren is closed-world (ds-label, ds-text-input, ds-checkbox all register as atoms before this molecule does); slot allowlists are load-bearing the same way they are for ds-button.

provenance.author: ai
provenance.justification: Design decision: validation-mode is read live via getComputedStyle(this).getPropertyValue('--ctx-validation-mode') at the moment validation runs (blur time or validate()-call time), never cached at construction or connectedCallback. Rejected alternative: reading the ctx token once in connectedCallback and caching it on the instance, which is simpler and avoids a getComputedStyle call on every blur. Rejected because a cached read breaks the demo's central claim (success-criteria.md P4c: 'the difference must be driven only by the ctx token') the moment a component is constructed once and then the page flips data-context afterward -- a cached value would keep validating in the mode it was born in instead of the mode it is currently in, which is exactly the 'context adaptation requires component-level branching' failure mode CLAUDE.md's success criteria names as a POC failure. A second design decision: `error` is a string the HOST sets (not text this component invents), so the message itself stays entirely under application/human control while the component only owns *when* to surface it -- rejected the alternative of a fixed built-in message ('This field is required') because it would silently misdescribe non-required-empty validity failures the pipeline can't yet express (this POC's validate() only checks emptiness, but the API contract should not bake that assumption into copy).
